info.name: JSONPlaceholder API Tests
info.description:
Manual QA testing project for the JSONPlaceholder REST API.

Covers 10 test cases across Users, Posts, and Comments — including  
GET and POST requests, positive and negative testing.

Tools: Postman, JSONPlaceholder API ([https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com](https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com))  
Result: 9 Passed, 1 Failed (see Bug Report for details)

request.description:
## GET /users

Retrieves a list of all users from the JSONPlaceholder API.

**JSONPlaceholder** is a free, fake REST API for testing and prototyping. No authentication is required to use this endpoint.

### Response

Returns an array of user objects. Each user object contains the following fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | Unique identifier for the user |
| `name` | string | Full name of the user |
| `username` | string | Username/handle |
| `email` | string | Email address |
| `address` | object | Address details (street, suite, city, zipcode, geo coordinates) |
| `phone` | string | Phone number |
| `website` | string | Personal website |
| `company` | object | Company details (name, catchPhrase, bs) |

## Get User by ID

Retrieves a single user by their unique ID from the JSONPlaceholder API.

**Method:** `GET`  
**URL:** `https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users/1`

### Path Parameter

| Parameter | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `1` | integer | The unique ID of the user to retrieve. In this request, `1` refers to the first user in the dataset. |

### Response

Returns a JSON object containing the details of the specified user. The response includes the following fields:

- **id** – The unique identifier of the user
    
- **name** – The full name of the user
    
- **username** – The user's username
    
- **email** – The user's email address
    
- **address** – An object containing street, suite, city, zipcode, and geo coordinates
    
- **phone** – The user's phone number
    
- **website** – The user's website
    
- **company** – An object containing the company name, catchPhrase, and bs
    

### Example Response

``` json
{
  "id": 1,
  "name": "Leanne Graham",
  "username": "Bret",
  "email": "Sincere@april.biz",
  "address": {
    "street": "Kulas Light",
    "suite": "Apt. 556",
    "city": "Gwenborough",
    "zipcode": "92998-3874",
    "geo": { "lat": "-37.3159", "lng": "81.1496" }
  },
  "phone": "1-770-736-8031 x56442",
  "website": "hildegard.org",
  "company": {
    "name": "Romaguera-Crona",
    "catchPhrase": "Multi-layered client-server neural-net",
    "bs": "harness real-time e-markets"
  }
}

 ```

Fetches a single user by their unique ID from the JSONPlaceholder API.

**Endpoint:** `GET /users/999`

**Purpose:** This request is used to test the **404 Not Found** error response. User ID `999` does not exist in JSONPlaceholder, so the API returns an empty object with a 404 status code.

**Expected Response:**

- **Status:** `404 Not Found`
    
- **Body:** `{}`
    

Use this request to validate that your application handles missing resources gracefully.

## Get All Posts

Retrieves a list of all posts from the JSONPlaceholder API.

**Base URL:** `https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts`

**Method:** `GET`

### Authentication

No authentication required.

### Response

Returns an array of post objects. Each post object contains the following fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | Unique identifier for the post |
| `title` | string | Title of the post |
| `body` | string | Body/content of the post |
| `userId` | integer | ID of the user who created the post |

### Example Response

``` json
[
  {
    "userId": 1,
    "id": 1,
    "title": "sunt aut facere repellat provident occaecati excepturi optio reprehenderit",
    "body": "quia et suscipit\nsuscipit recusandae consequuntur expedita et cum\nreprehenderit molestiae ut ut quas totam\nnostrum rerum est autem sunt rem eveniet architecto"
  }
]

 ```

## Get Post by ID

Retrieves a single post by its ID from the [JSONPlaceholder](https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com) API — a free fake REST API for testing and prototyping.

---

### Endpoint

**GET** `https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1`

---

### Path Parameter

| Parameter | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | `1` | The unique identifier of the post to retrieve |

---

### Expected Response

**Status:** `200 OK`

The response is a JSON object representing a single post:

``` json
{
  "id": 1,
  "userId": 1,
  "title": "sunt aut facere repellat provident occaecati excepturi optio reprehenderit",
  "body": "quia et suscipit\nsuscipit recusandae consequuntur expedita et cum\nreprehenderit molestiae ut ut quas totam\nnostrum rerum est autem sunt rem eveniet architecto"
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | The unique ID of the post |
| `userId` | integer | The ID of the user who created the post |
| `title` | string | The title of the post |
| `body` | string | The body/content of the post |

## Create a New Post

This request creates a new post by sending a `POST` request to the `/posts` endpoint of the JSONPlaceholder API.

### Request Body

The request body must be sent as raw JSON with the following fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `title` | String | The title of the post |
| `body` | String | The main content/body text of the post |
| `userId` | Integer | The ID of the user who is creating the post |

**Example:**

``` json
{
  "title": "My First Post",
  "body": "This is the content of the post.",
  "userId": 1
}

 ```

### Successful Response

On success, the API returns a `201 Created` status code along with the newly created post object, including a server-assigned `id`:

``` json
{
  "id": 101,
  "title": "My First Post",
  "body": "This is the content of the post.",
  "userId": 1
}

 ```

## GET /comments

Retrieves a list of all comments associated with posts from the JSONPlaceholder fake REST API.

### Base URL

`https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com`

### HTTP Method

`GET`

### Endpoint

`/comments`

### Response Format

Returns a **JSON array** of comment objects. Each object contains the following fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | Unique identifier for the comment |
| `postId` | integer | ID of the post this comment belongs to |
| `name` | string | Title/subject of the comment |
| `email` | string | Email address of the commenter |
| `body` | string | The content/body of the comment |

### Example Response

``` json
[
  {
    "postId": 1,
    "id": 1,
    "name": "id labore ex et quam laborum",
    "email": "Eliseo@gardner.biz",
    "body": "laudantium enim quasi est quidem magnam voluptate ipsam eos\ntempora quo necessitatibus\ndolor quam autem quasi\nreiciendis et nam sapiente accusantium"
  },
  ...
]

 ```

Attempts to retrieve a user by ID from the JSONPlaceholder API using the endpoint `GET /users/:id`.

**About this request:**

- The ID used here is `abc`, which is a **non-numeric and invalid** user ID. JSONPlaceholder only recognizes integer IDs (e.g., `1` through `10` for users).
    
- Since no user with the ID `abc` exists in the dataset, this request is expected to return an **empty object (****`{}`****)** with a `200 OK` status, or a `404 Not Found` response, depending on how the API handles unrecognized IDs.
    

**Use case:** This request is useful for testing how your client or application handles invalid or unexpected ID formats — for example, verifying error handling logic when a non-numeric value is passed where an integer is required.

## GET /posts/:id

Retrieves a single post by its unique ID from the JSONPlaceholder API.

### Path Parameter

| Parameter | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | `1` | The unique identifier of the post to retrieve |

### Successful Response

**Status:** `200 OK`

**Response Body** (JSON object):

``` json
{
  "id": 1,
  "title": "sunt aut facere repellat provident occaecati excepturi optio reprehenderit",
  "body": "quia et suscipit\nsuscipit recusandae consequuntur expedita et cum\nreprehenderit molestiae ut ut quas totam\nnostrum rerum est autem sunt rem eveniet architecto",
  "userId": 1
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | Unique identifier of the post |
| `title` | string | Title of the post |
| `body` | string | Full text content of the post |
| `userId` | integer | ID of the user who created the post |

**Test Case:** Create post with empty body (Negative Test)

**Method:** POST  
**Endpoint:** /posts  
**Body:** Empty (no data sent)

**Expected Result:** Status 400 Bad Request — API should reject empty/missing required fields (title, body, userId)

**Actual Result:** Status 201 Created — API accepted the empty body and returned {"id": 101} with no validation error

**Status:** Fail ❌

**Bug Reference:** BUG-01 — POST /posts accepts empty body without validation
